atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports right foot pain

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports pain

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports the pain began approximately two days ago following a fall while playing tennis

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports he fell on his foot attempting a double volley

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports he wrapped the foot and iced it

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports experiencing pain

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports difficulty bearing weight

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports ibuprofen provided some relief

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient denies prior injuries to this foot

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient denies a previous injury to his left leg

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports he was unable to continue playing tennis after the injury

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports he was assisted off the field

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports no numbness or tingling

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Physical exam reveals bruising to the plantar and dorsal aspects of the right foot.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The foot is swollen and warm to the touch.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Palpation elicits tenderness at the midfoot.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The Lisfranc joint exam reveals a positive piano key test at the first and second metatarsal levels.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Capillary refill is less than three seconds in the right foot's dorsalis pedis pulse.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Neurological exam is intact, with no sensory deficits.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Vitals are stable: BP 120/75, HR 60, RR 18, Temp 97.1°F, SpO2 100%.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Right foot pain consistent with a Lisfranc fracture, involving a disruption of the Lisfranc joint.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician prescribes surgical intervention.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: A clinical order is placed for an MRI.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician prescribes a cast.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician prescribes crutches.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient will follow up in one week.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: A follow-up appointment is scheduled in six to eight weeks.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician provides documentation regarding the risks and benefits of surgery and the potential complications.
